message:
refactor(upgrade): extract render_not_updated helper + case-insensitive archive extension match

Two small follow-ups to the SHA256-verification landing:

1. Extract `render_not_updated(current_version, latest_version, ctx,
   is_json, is_quiet, is_rich)` from the duplicated "already up to
   date" blocks. Both `execute_upgrade` short-circuits — the
   no-release-selected branch and the `!update_available` branch —
   were building an identical `UpdateResult` and dispatching through
   the same json / quiet / rich / plain matrix. Folding them into one
   helper removes the duplication and makes any future tweak to the
   "no update" rendering a single-edit change instead of two.

2. `is_archive_asset_name` now lowercases the asset name before
   checking suffixes. GitHub releases occasionally mix case in asset
   names (e.g. `.TAR.GZ` from some automated release pipelines), and
   the strict-case predicate would have silently filtered those
   archives out of the candidate list, then complained about a
   missing release archive. The exclusions for `.sha256` and
   `.minisig` are also lowercased so casing can't smuggle a sidecar
   into the archive list.

3. Cosmetic rustfmt tweak inside the `checksum_asset` error branch:
   bind `let expected_checksum_name = format!("{}.sha256", ...)` into
   a local before passing to the outer `format!`. Avoids a nested
   `format!` inside `format!` flagged by clippy's
   `format_in_format_args` on recent toolchains.

No behaviour change outside the casing fix; everything else is a pure
refactor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
